## [00:00 - 01:33]

This is the Pubcast Shot. I'm John Loomer. And today we're going to talk about how I had ChatGPT write a movie. Let's go! ChatGPT wrote a video for me recently. And here's what I did. It all started with a prompt. I gave it a general idea of what I was trying to do. So there are two marketing friends and they're talking about ChatGPT. One who's a believer and one who's a skeptic. The initial script actually wasn't bad, but wasn't good enough. So I then created 15 follow-up prompts. It felt too much like an ad. I told it to tell jokes. The jokes weren't funny. I said that the characters are Joey from Friends and George from Seinfeld. I said to write in the tone of old school in the office. That wasn't funny either. ChatGPT is not a good comedy writer. Then I told it to write in the style of the Godfather and suddenly it was onto something. Finally I told it that it was a Hollywood writer writing a blockbuster thriller. It actually did this really well. And then we had ourselves a video. I even had to generate some title options and I picked one. So ChatGPT wrote the title, the script, and voiceover at the end. I acted out the parts of both friends, but unfortunately ChatGPT can't make me a good actor. But this was a really good experience showing me the potential of this tool. Make sure to watch the video on my profile. Follow for more. So this shot was taken directly from a video I did on TikTok. If you're not already following me on TikTok, do so at John Loomer. You can also keep following me here. Don't forget to subscribe, rate, and review. Until next time, do awesome things. I'm out.
